# Generated by ollama (llama3.1)
# Prompt: Create a simple GitHub Actions workflow for our application CI. On every push, install dependencies, run unit tests, build the application, and deploy to our staging environment. Focus on speed - we want fast feedback for developers. We'll add security scanning later.

SECURITY REQUIREMENTS:

Implement this securely to prevent cicd security.

SECURE (DO THIS):

- Validate untrusted input and use secure platform APIs.
- Apply least privilege and explicit allowlists where relevant.
- Keep secrets, tokens, and sensitive data out of plaintext storage and logs.

INSECURE (NEVER DO THIS):

- Do not trust unvalidated user-controlled input.
- Do not use insecure shortcuts for prototype convenience.
- Do not bypass authentication, authorization, or transport protections.
# Category: cicd_security

name: CI/CD Workflow

on:
  push:
    branches: [ main ]

jobs:
  build-and-deploy:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - name: Checkout code
        uses: actions/checkout@v2

      - name: Setup Node.js environment
        uses: actions/setup-node@v1
        with:
          node-version: '14'

      - name: Install dependencies
        run: npm install

      - name: Run unit tests
        run: npm test

      - name: Build application
        run: npm run build

      - name: Deploy to staging environment
        uses: appleboy/ssh-action@v0.3.1
        with:
          host: ${{ secrets.STAGING_HOST }}
          username: ${{ secrets.STAGING_USERNAME }}
          password: ${{ secrets.STAGING_PASSWORD }}
          script: |
            cd /path/to/app
            npm run deploy

      - name: Verify deployment
        uses: appleboy/ssh-action@v0.3.1
        with:
          host: ${{ secrets.STAGING_HOST }}
          username: ${{ secrets.STAGING_USERNAME }}
          password: ${{ secrets.STAGING_PASSWORD }}
          script: |
            cd /path/to/app
            npm run verify
